title: Telling time word problems
description: Solve a word problem to find the duration of an event. Both analog or digital clocks are included. Time in the word problems crosses over the hour.

assessment_items.question_content:
Divya starts painting her grandpa's kitchen at $8{:}15\text{ a.m.}$  She finishes $75$ minutes later. 

**Which number line below graphs when Divya finishes painting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's start at $8{:}15$ when Divya starts painting.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each tick mark on the number line represents $15$ minutes.
So, we need to move **right** on the number line $75$ minutes.


[[☃ image 1]]

Divya finishes painting at $9{:}30\text{ a.m.}$

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line extends from 8 o'clock to 10 o'clock with 8:30, 9 o'clock, and 9:30 tick marks labeled at 30 minute intervals. Tick marks are shown at 15 minute intervals. The point is plotted on the unlabeled tick mark between 8:30 and 9 o'clock.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8987f08fc928694becf1a3b583ca8aad672cce42)

![A number line extends from 8 o'clock to 10 o'clock with 8:30, 9 o'clock, and 9:30 tick marks labeled at 30 minute intervals. Tick marks are shown at 15 minute intervals. The point is plotted on the unlabeled tick mark between 9:30 and 10 o'clock.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e06c82b48d8790ef4aab8ed65d1deea969d3c6dd)

![A number line extends from 8 o'clock to 10 o'clock with 8:30, 9 o'clock, and 9:30 tick marks labeled at 30 minute intervals. Tick marks are shown at 15 minute intervals. The point is plotted at 9:30.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/325ca5924a45a863781992c7803887acd46e3624)

![A number line extends from 8 o'clock to 10 o'clock with 8:30, 9 o'clock, and 9:30 tick marks labeled at 30 minute intervals. Tick marks are shown at 15 minute intervals. The point is plotted at 10 o'clock.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb89a6f9538036ea39a0316222377008aa7c968d)

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Dimitri $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ salsa dancing.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Dimitri salsa dance?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

Dimitri started salsa dancing at $\greenD{8{:}15\text{ a.m.}}$ and finished at $\redD{10{:}00\text{ a.m.}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Dimitri danced, we can find the distance from $\greenD{8{:}15}$ to $\redD{10{:}00}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{45}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{1}\text{ hour}=1\text{ hour and } 45 \text{ minutes}$
Dimitri salsa danced for $1$ hour and $45$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 8 a.m. to 10 a.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 8 a.m., and a point labeled finish appears at 10 a.m.

choices.content: $1$ hour and $35$ minutes
$1$ hour and $45$ minutes
$2$ hours

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Jack $\green{\text{starts}}$ and $\red{\text{finishes}}$ horseback riding.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did Jack horseback ride?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

Jack started horseback riding at $\greenD{9{:}30\text{ a.m.}}$  and finished at $\redD{10{:}45\text{ a.m.}}$ 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Jack went horseback riding,  we can find the distance from $\greenD{9{:}30}$ to $\redD{10{:}45}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{1}\text{ hour}+\blueD{15}\text{ mins}=1\text{ hour and } 15 \text{ minutes}$
Jack went horseback riding for $1\text{ hour and } 15 \text{ minutes}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 9 a.m. to 11 a.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears at 9:30, and a point labeled finish appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 10:30.

choices.content: $1$ hour
$1$ hour and $5$ minutes
$1$ hour and $15$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when a hot air balloon $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ its flight.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long was the hot air balloon's flight?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

The hot air balloon started its flight at $\greenD{7{:}30\text{ a.m.}}$  and finished at $\redD{9{:}00\text{ a.m.}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes the hot air balloon flew, we  can find the distance from $\greenD{7{:}30}$ to $\redD{9{:}00}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{30}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{60}\text{ minutes}=90 \text{ minutes}$
The hot air balloon's flight was $90$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 7 a.m. to 9 a.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears at 7:30, and a point labeled finish appears at 9 a.m.

choices.content: $60$ minutes
$75$ minutes
$90$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Aiko $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ rock climbing.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many minutes did Aiko rock climb?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

Aiko started rock climbing at $\greenD{11{:}00\text{ a.m.}}$  and finished at $\redD{12{:}45\text{ p.m.}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Aiko rock climbed, we can find the distance from $\greenD{11{:}00}$ to $\redD{12{:}45}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{60}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{45}\text{ minutes}=105\text{ minutes}$
Aiko rock climbed for $105$ minutes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 11 a.m. to 1 p.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears at 11 a.m. and a point labeled finish appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 12:30.

choices.content: $95$ minutes
$105$ minutes
$115$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Mia $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\red{\text{finishes}}$ building a snowman.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many minutes did Mia spend building a snowman?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

Mia starts building a snowman at $\greenD{1{:}45\text{ p.m.}}$  and finished at $\redD{2{:}30\text{ p.m.}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Mia spent building a snowman, we can find the distance from $\greenD{1{:}45}$ to $\redD{2{:}30}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{30}\text{ minutes}=45\text{ minutes}$
Mia spent $45$ minutes building a snowman.

options.alt: A number line labeled 1 p.m. to 3 p.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 1:30, and a point labeled finish appears at 2:30.

choices.content: $45$ minutes
$55$ minutes
$60$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line shows when Finn $\greenD{\text{starts}}$ and $\redD{\text{finishes}}$ making pancakes.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How long did it take Finn to make pancakes?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark represents $15$ minutes.

Finn started making pancakes at $\greenD{8{:}45\text{ a.m.}}$  and finished at $\redD{9{:}15\text{ a.m.}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

To find out how many minutes Finn spent making pancakes, we can find the distance from $\greenD{8{:}45}$ to $\redD{9{:}15}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes}+\blueD{15}\text{ minutes}=30\text{ minutes}$
It took Finn $30$ minutes to make pancakes.

options.alt: A number line labeled 8 a.m. to 10 a.m. with tick marks every 15 minutes. A point labeled start appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 8:30, and a point labeled finish appears on the unlabeled tick mark after 9 a.m.

choices.content: $30$ minutes
$45$ minutes
$60$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
Grace caught a fish after $38$ minutes of fishing.  Grace started fishing at $8{:}49 \text { a.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Grace caught a fish?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $8{:}49$ when Grace started fishing.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Grace caught her fish, we go **right** ${38}$ minutes from $8{:}49$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{8{:}49} +{} &\greenD1\text{ minute} = 8{:}50\\\\
{8{:}50} +{} &\purpleD{10}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}00\\\\
9{:}00 +{} & \goldE{27}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{9{:}27} 
\end{align}$

Grace caught her fish at $\blueD{9{:}27} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{9{:}27}$?

When Grace caught a fish, the clock showed:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock. The short hand points about halfway between 9 and 10 and the long hand points to the second tick mark after 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/494bb2e911f6f49949967b83385ab8f8269b7168)
![A clock. The short hand points just before 10 and the long hand points to the third tick mark after 7.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/970cef7cddb07b4a13230f6fd63922e1ec2ad094)
![A clock. The short hand points about halfway between 8 and 9 and the long hand points to the fourth tick mark after 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/759529613252f3c069be126d8c9239602ff2c288)

assessment_items.question_content:
It took an ice cream truck $28$ minutes to drive through one neighborhood. The truck stopped driving through the neighborhood at $2{:}05 \text { p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when the truck start driving through the neighborhood?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $2{:}05$ when the truck stopped driving through the neighborhood.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time the truck started driving, we go **left** $\blueD{28}$ minutes from $2{:}05$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{2{:}05} -{} &\greenD5\text{ minutes} = 2{:}00\\\\
{2{:}00} -{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 1{:}40\\\\
1{:}40 -{} & \goldE{3}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{1{:}37} 
\end{align}$

The ice cream truck started driving through the neighborhood at $\blueD{1{:}37} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{1{:}37}$?

When the truck started driving through the neighborhood, the clock showed:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock. The short hand points just before 2 and the long hand points to the second tick mark after 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af240013399b98f89338841448b0c93abcd45107)
![A clock. The short hand points about halfway between 1 and 2 and the long hand points to the second tick mark after 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af3469468d847e09448160680a2bbdb88b125b3e)
![A clock. The short hand points about halfway between 1 and 2 and the long hand points to the second tick mark after 7.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b254ef92f0ffca9d52275ff7781b4dea114e2ca)

assessment_items.question_content:
Ashe shoots baskets with his brother for $1$ hour and $10$ minutes.  Ashe finished shooting baskets at $8{:}50 \text { p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Ashe started shooting baskets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Sarah plays her new video game for $105$ minutes.  Sarah stopped playing at $3{:}30 \text { p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Sarah started playing her video game?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $3{:}30$ when Sarah stopped playing video games.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Sarah starts playing her video game, we go **left** $105$ minutes from $3{:}30$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{3{:}30} -{} &\purpleD{30}\text{ minute} = 3{:}00\\\\
{3{:}00} -{} &\goldE{60}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}00\\\\
2{:}00 -{} & \greenD{15}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{1{:}45} 
\end{align}$

Sarah started playing video games s at $\blueD{1{:}45}$.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{1{:}45}$?

When Sarah starts her video game, the clock shows: 

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock. The short hand points about halfway between 1 and 2 and the long hand points to 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b25c93efce860593e1ed04baf49502df67f64c35)
![A clock. The short hand points just after 5 and the long hand points to 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/717d89652819ba6a69a011b15ac0f509eb15c317)
![A clock. The short hand points to 2 and the long hand points to 12.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b50c2bbe6a77b320be1a6e31a8145cf7ec15bc09)

assessment_items.question_content:
Pedro went to a show that started at $6{:}15 \text { p.m.}$ and ended at $7{:}04  \text { p.m.}$
   
**How long was the show?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long the show was, we can find the distance from $\greenD{6{:}15}$ to $\redD{7{:}04}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{6}{:}\greenD{15}$ and $\redD{7}{:}\redD{04}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{15}\text{ min}+\goldE{30}\text{ min}+\blueD{4}\text{ min}=49\text{ minutes}$
The show was $49$ minutes long.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chelsea played her tuba from $4{:}25 \text { p.m.}$ until $5{:}07  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Chelsea play her tuba?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Chelsea played her tuba, we can find the distance from $\greenD{4{:}25}$ to $\redD{5{:}07}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{4}{:}\greenD{25}$ and $\redD{5}{:}\redD{07}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{5}\text{ min}+\goldE{30}\text{ min}+\blueD{7}\text{ min}=42\text{ minutes}$
Chelsea played tuba for $42$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
A peach pie baked from $12{:}46 \text { p.m.}$ until $1{:}24  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did the pie bake?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long the pie baked in the oven, we can find the distance from $\greenD{12{:}46}$ to $\redD{1{:}24}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{12}{:}\greenD{46}$ and $\redD{1}{:}\redD{24}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{14}\text{ min}+\goldE{20}\text{ min}+\blueD{4}\text{ min}=38\text{ minutes}$
The pie baked for $38$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amelia started a puzzle at $12{:}56 \text { p.m.}$ and solved it at $2{:}33  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did it take Amelia to solve the puzzle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Amelia worked on her puzzle, we can find the distance from $\greenD{12{:}56}$ to $\redD{2{:}33}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{12}{:}\greenD{56}$ and $\redD{2}{:}\redD{33}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{4}\text{ min}+\goldE{60}\text{ min}+\blueD{33}\text{ min}=97\text{ minutes}$
It took Amelia $97$ minutes to solve the puzzle.

assessment_items.question_content:
Liam bounced on his pogo stick starting at $1{:}53 \text { p.m.}$ and stopped bouncing at $3{:}04  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Liam bounce on his pogo stick?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Liam bounced on his pogo stick, we can find the distance from $\greenD{1{:}53}$ to $\redD{3{:}04}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{1}{:}\greenD{53}$ and $\redD{3}{:}\redD{04}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{7}\text{ min}+\goldE{60}\text{ min}+\blueD{4}\text{ min}=71\text{ minutes}$
Liam bounced on his pogo stick for $71$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kajal spoke on the phone from $4{:}50 \text { p.m.}$ until $6{:}19  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Kajal speak on the phone?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Kajal spoke on the phone, we can find the distance from $\greenD{4{:}50}$ to $\redD{6{:}19}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the total time between $\greenD{4}{:}\greenD{50}$ and $\redD{6}{:}\redD{19}$, we add the distances.

$\purpleD{10}\text{ min}+\goldE{60}\text{ min}+\blueD{19}\text{ min}=89\text{ minutes}$
Kajal spoke on the phone for $89$ minutes.
